patch.value:
## Context

`src/core/history/drift-scan.ts:19` writes `DRIFT_CACHE_FILENAME = 'history-drift-cache.json'` to `runtime/`. The gc runtime scope target at `src/cli/commands/gc.ts:41-48` only includes `runtime/test-runs`.

If an operator manually edits a history JSONL file (e.g. to repair after an external tool), the mtime/ctime/size/content-hash in the drift cache will be stale. The health scan will re-verify on content-hash mismatch, but `pm gc --scope runtime` fails to purge the cache as expected.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Add `{ scope: 'runtime', relativePath: 'runtime/history-drift-cache.json', kind: 'file' }` to `GC_TARGETS`
- [ ] `pm gc --scope runtime` removes the drift cache (triggers a fresh full-scan on next `pm health`)
- [ ] GC guidance updated to mention that removing the drift cache forces a full re-scan
- [ ] Unit test covering the new target path

## Evidence

- `src/cli/commands/gc.ts:41-48` — `runtime` GC_TARGET only covers `runtime/test-runs`
- `src/core/history/drift-scan.ts:19` — cache path is `runtime/history-drift-cache.json`
- `src/core/history/drift-scan.ts:36-49` — `loadDriftCache` reads from this path
Drift-cache gc scope: pm gc --scope runtime should clear history-drift-cache.json

The drift verification cache at .agents/pm/runtime/history-drift-cache.json is not covered by pm gc --scope runtime. The runtime scope target list in gc.ts only covers runtime/test-runs/. A stale drift cache after manual history edits can cause pm health to report false-clean results until it naturally invalidates.

- Add `{ scope: 'runtime', relativePath: 'runtime/history-drift-cache.json', kind: 'file' }` to `GC_TARGETS`
- `pm gc --scope runtime` removes the drift cache (triggers a fresh full-scan on next `pm health`)
- GC guidance updated to mention that removing the drift cache forces a full re-scan
- Unit test covering the new target path
